items.title: Teenage boy dies after stolen car rolls on major NT highway

children.content: A teenager has died after the stolen vehicle he was inside rolled on a major Northern Territory highway in the early hours of this morning.

gallery.altText: A vehicle parked across the highway, blocking traffic access, with a logo on the side that reads: ROAD OPERATIONS

thumbnail.altText: A vehicle parked across the highway, blocking traffic access, with a logo on the side that reads: ROAD OPERATIONS

items.title: NT gas workers strike as negotiations on pay deal stall

children.content: Australian workers at the Japanese gas company Inpex walked off the job this morning, and plan to strike again this evening.

image.altText: Evening / dusk shot of Darwin harbour, industrial site, with yellow lights glowing and cranes in view.

items.title: Residents can't eat too many fish from river in PFAS-affected town

children.content: Katherine residents have welcomed a $2 billion lawsuit over PFAS contamination in the town, saying "it's about time we started holding large corporations to account".

image.altText: Anjali Palmer stands outside a medical clinic in Katherine.

items.title: Historic journey along Overland Telegraph Line captures the imagination

children.content: If you saw a car in the central Australian outback you wouldn't expect it to be a vintage 1925 Studebaker ... let alone two of them, driven by two men called Brenton.

gallery.altText: Two men in blue shirts smile for a selfie inside a vintage Studebaker. The man on the left wears a hat and sunnies.

thumbnail.altText: Two men in blue shirts smile for a selfie inside a vintage Studebaker. The man on the left wears a hat and sunnies.

items.title: NT WorkSafe charges health department over hospital aerosol can death

children.content: WorkSafe charges Northern Territory Health with failing to comply with health and safety duties over the death of a mental health in-patient at Royal Darwin Hospital in 2022.

image.altText: Emergency department of hospital with sunset behind it

items.title: Search for man missing in East Arnhem Land ends after nine days

children.content: After a nine-day operation covering more than 1,300 square kilometres of land, the search for a 31-year-old man in East Arnhem Land has been stood down.

image.altText: A Northern Territory police vehicle in Katherine.

items.title: US Navy interested in Darwin ship lift, NT minister says

children.content: Northern Territory Infrastructure Minister Bill Yan says the United States Navy has expressed interest in using the controversial Darwin ship lift, which is expected to be operational next year.

image.altText: Construction site with several cranes on a harbour

items.title: Decades-old trees in Darwin finally flower for the first, and only, time

children.content: About 20 Gebang palms have flowered in Darwin in a rare spectacle, reaffirming the local council's plans to foster a new generation of the trees.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a Gebang palm tree in flower, with small yellow tufts growing outward.

items.title: Neighbours complain about 200 chopper flights a week into luxury resort

children.content: A luxury resort that has been shut for three years hopes to reopen by chauffeuring guests down a steep 4WD track and flying them in on more than 200 helicopter flights a week.

gallery.altText: A building with the lights on, on a green slope next to a mountain shrouded in clouds.

thumbnail.altText: A building with the lights on, on a green slope next to a mountain shrouded in clouds.

items.title: James Boag ends 145-year history as brewer ceases Tasmanian production

children.content: The Iconic northern Tasmanian brewer announces it will cease production in the state by November and repay a government loan it secured to keep its Launceston brewery visitor centre open.

image.altText: James Boags Beers 2024-06-25 10:06:00

items.title: How batteries muscled out gas and pushed power prices down

children.content: Fossil fuels, which have powered the global economy for centuries, are under threat. And Australia has become ground zero in this global battle.

image.altText: Solar panels on rooftop

items.title: Trump rejects Iranian reports of peace talk suspension

children.content: Iranian state media is reporting that the country's government has halted negotiations with the United States over an end to the war in the Middle East, in protest over Israel's conduct in Lebanon.

image.altText: Smoke rises over a town.

items.title: Offers made for Whitsundays resort 'comparable to Hamilton Island'

children.content: A proposed resort and overwater gondola at a Whitsundays island is a step closer to becoming reality, after attracting expressions of interest from several possible buyers.

image.altText: A passenger cabin with glass windows is lifted over an island resort by overhead cables.

items.title: Tax deduction red flags on the ATO's radar

children.content: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) is warning Australians to stop claiming private expenses on their tax returns. The agency gives its tips this tax time, including the dos and don'ts of claiming work-related expenses and investment losses.

gallery.altText: A tax return form with red flag pins in it

thumbnail.altText: A tax return form with red flag pins in it

items.title: Workmate of missing Jack McLennan says he's 'given police absolutely everything'

children.content: The last known person to see missing Queensland man Jack McLennan alive has broken his public silence amid intense community speculation over his role in Mr McLennan's disappearance.

image.altText: Chris Duke wearing a hi-vis yellow shirt and sunglasses

items.title: Union claims under-resourcing led to delayed Melbourne fatal fire response

children.content: The United Firefighters Union says the only fire truck at the nearest station had already been called to a job, leading to a delayed response to a fire that killed a three-year-old boy.

image.altText: Police tape outside a burnt house.

items.title: Race to develop Ebola vaccine as deadly strain of virus spreads

children.content: Nearly 250 people are suspected to have died, and more than 1,100 are infected with the rare strain of Ebola in the DRC and Uganda, though the true spread of the virus is feared to be much wider.

image.altText: Five health care workers wearing PPE, facemasks and eyemasks standing lined up out the front of a home.

items.title: Owning a dog wasn't like I'd imagined

children.content: I completely neglected to give much thought to whether my personality would slot into a dog's lifestyle. Spoiler — it did not.

image.altText: Bruce smiles as he holds his cattle cross dog Steady.

items.title: Quiz: Are you smart enough to understand the Scripps Spelling Bee?

children.content: We don't think you'll have even heard of most of these words, let alone know how to spell them.

gallery.altText: A teenage boy holds a trophy on his head, next to an event official

thumbnail.altText: A teenage boy holds a trophy on his head, next to an event official

items.title: Why make-up sex may not be fixing anything

children.content: Experts say if a fight keeps ending up in the bedroom, it could be a sign you and your partner aren't resolving anything.

image.altText: A couples legs lying in bed

items.title: NDAs to be waived for landmark military sexual violence inquiry

children.content: Gag orders imposed on former and current Defence personnel who experienced sexual violence will not be enforced, allowing them to participate in a landmark military sexual violence inquiry.

image.altText: A close-up of an Australian Army patch and an Australian flag patch on an ADF officer's uniform.

items.title: Twenty richest Australians hold more wealth than bottom 3 million households

children.content: Australia now has 178 billionaires who collectively hold more than $686 billion in wealth, sparking debate about the advantages and disadvantages of the growing number of ultra-wealthy.

gallery.altText: moody image of 50- and 100-dollar notes laid out over bank statements

thumbnail.altText: moody image of 50- and 100-dollar notes laid out over bank statements

items.title: Under-16 e-scooter ban watered down by Qld government

children.content: Under the amended bill, children aged between 12 and 17 will be allowed to ride-mobility devices if they're supervised.

image.altText: A close-up view of e-scooter wheels.

items.title: French Open Sabalenka's for the taking as draw opens up for number one

children.content: A series of upsets across Roland Garros leaves world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the box seat to win the claycourt grand slam after beating Naomi Osaka.

image.altText: Aryna Sabalenka blows a kiss to the French Open crowd.

items.title: 'Excited to be back': Serena Williams announces return to pro-tennis at 44

children.content: The greatest women's champion of the Open Era is returning to tennis after almost four years away, accepting a wildcard into the doubles at the Queen's Club tournament next week.

gallery.altText: Serena Williams clenches her fist during a tennis match at Wimbledon.

thumbnail.altText: Serena Williams clenches her fist during a tennis match at Wimbledon.

items.title: Antibiotic found in wild seafood 14 kilometres from salmon farm

children.content: Tasmania's Natural Resources and Environment Department has confirmed the antibiotic florfenicol was detected at a 'low level' in wild seafood 14 kilometres from where the drug was administered.

image.altText: Tassal salmon pens in Macquarie Harbour, southwest Tasmania.

items.title: UK's ex-US envoy slams 'bereft' Starmer leadership in new document drop

children.content: Among the more than 1,500 pages of documents were messages from Peter Mandelson voicing strong criticisms of Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer's leadership of the UK government.

image.altText: A close up of Keir Starmer's emotionless ace, while wearing dark-rimmed glasses and a blue shirt.

items.title: Real estate agency using fake AI salesperson to spam customers

children.content: "Daniel" is an AI real estate agent who calls strangers and secretly records conversations without their consent.

gallery.altText: A stern woman holds a phone

thumbnail.altText: A stern woman holds a phone

items.title: The impact of Australia only buying second-hand submarines under AUKUS

children.content: Australia will only buy second-hand Virginia nuclear-powered submarines from the US during the AUKUS stopgap phase, with some analysts criticising the move and others believing it is a positive change of plan.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: Police release CCTV footage of Melbourne shooting incident

children.content: A man was filmed firing three shots at a house in Melbourne's north in what police believe was a targeted incident last year.

image.altText: A man pointing a gun on CCTV.

items.title: Ex-soldier, comedian and aspiring Martian is the Aussie face of Laos cave rescue

children.content: Josh Richards has made news for his diving and his interplanetary ambitions. Now he's using his skills to try to rescue others.

gallery.altText: A cave diver wearing a yellow helmet, blue mask and black wetsuit squeezes between rocks.

thumbnail.altText: A cave diver wearing a yellow helmet, blue mask and black wetsuit squeezes between rocks.

items.title: Parent 'thought it was a joke' upon hearing year 3 NAPLAN tests stolen

children.content: Dozens of primary school students are victims of an analogue data breach after their NAPLAN tests were stolen from a van in Melbourne's inner-north on April Fool's Day.

image.altText: two school girls write in a book during class.

items.title: 'A fifth of the price': Council uses AI for 10-year strategic plan

children.content: A Tasmanian council says using AI for a strategic plan has saved them time and money, and will be used much more in the future, but a resident says the community consultation is 'completely lost with AI'.

image.altText: An aerial view of a regional city with hills in the distance under an overcast, electric blue sky at sunset.
